role_summary: SNCC field organizer and voter-registration project director in Hale County, Alabama and Natchez, Mississippi.
biographical_paragraph: Annie Pearl Avery was born in 1943 in Birmingham, Alabama; she was raised in Pittsburgh from age nine and returned to Birmingham at age fourteen [src: 1]. As a Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee field organizer she served as project director for voter registration in Hale County, Alabama, and staffed the Natchez, Mississippi project [src: 1]. She participated in a 1961 Woolworth's sit-in organized by Reverend Fred Shuttlesworth, worked with Julian Bond and James Forman during a SNCC conference in Georgia in 1961, and was present on the Edmund Pettus Bridge during the March 7, 1965 events known as Bloody Sunday [src: 1]. Avery is on record as carrying weapons during Mississippi voter registration work and questioning the effectiveness of strict nonviolence as a movement tactic [src: 1]. Her oral history is held by the Library of Congress as entry 3 of the Civil Rights History Project [src: 2].

sources.title: Annie Pearl Avery oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress
